FEE PAID FOR (1) DEFENDANT SW HOTEL, INCS OBJECTIONS TO EVIDENCE AND DECLARATION OF YONG ZHI PAN SUBMITTED IN SUPPORT OF PLAINTIFFS OPPOSITION TO DEFENDANTS MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT; (2) [PROPOSED] ORDER THEREON (TRANSACTION ID # 58357272) FILED BY DEFENDANT SW HOTEL, INC. DBA SAM WONG HOTEL

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SW HOTEL, INC. DBA SAM WONG HOTEL'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT HAS MET ITS BURDEN OF SHOWING THAT ONE OR MORE OF THE ELEMENTS OF THE CAUSE OF ACTIONS ALLEGED BY PLAINTIFF CANNOT BE ESTABLISHED. (CCP SECTION 437C(O)(2). WHATEVER THE PARTICULAR BASIS OF LIABILITY URGED BY A PLAINTIFF, IN THE ABSENCE OF DIRECT MANAGEMENT OVER THE MEANS AND METHODS OF THE INDEPENDENT CONTRACTOR'S WORK OR THE PROVISION OF THE EQUIPMENT WHICH CAUSED THE INJURY, NO LEGAL DUTY ON THE OWNER OR HIRER IS CREATED. (ZAMUDIO V. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO (1999) 70 CAL.APP.4TH 445, 453.) PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO SUBMIT EVIDENCE THAT RAISES A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT AS TO WHETHER DEFENDANT CONTROLLED THE MEANS AND METHODS OF THE WORK. (SEPARATE STATEMENT OF DISPUTED AND UNDISPUTED MATERIAL FACTS # 5 AND 7.) PLAINTIFF'S CONTENTION THAT BECAUSE DEFENDANT DID NOT PROVIDE OR PAY FOR THE FIXED SCAFFOLDING DEFENDANT WAS CONTROLLING THE TYPE OF SCAFFOLDING THAT WAS TO BE USED ON THE JOBSITE, IS SPECULATIVE AND IS NOT SUPPORTED BY EVIDENCE. THUS, DEFENDANT IS ENTITLED TO SUMMARY JUDGMENT. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO, CSR #7489. (302/EG)

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-22-2016 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Feb-08-2016 Set For Case Management Conference On May-11-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment By Defendant Sw Hotel, Inc.
Off Calendar Jan-04-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Sze-Hang Lo of Law Offices of S.H. Lo telephone number 510-228-5388 for Plaintiff.
No other appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

Mr. Lo presented oral argument. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT SW HOTEL, INC. DBA SAM WONG HOTEL'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. Defendant has met its burden of showing that one or more of the elements of the cause of actions alleged by Plaintiff cannot be established. (CCP section 437c(o)(2). Whatever the particular basis of liability urged by a plaintiff, in the absence of direct management over the means and methods of the independent contractor's work or the provision of the equipment which caused the injury, no legal duty on the owner or hirer is created. (Zamudio v. City and County of San Francisco (1999) 70 Cal.App.4th 445, 453.) Plaintiff has failed to submit evidence that raises a triable issue of material fact as to whether Defendant controlled the means and methods of the work. (Separate Statement of Disputed and Undisputed Material Facts # 5 and 7.) Plaintiff's contention that because Defendant did not provide or pay for the fixed scaffolding Defendant was controlling the type of scaffolding that was to be used on the jobsite, is speculative and is not supported by evidence. Thus, Defendant is entitled to summary judgment. Order signed.

Judge: Ernest Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489. (302/EG)

MOTION TO COMPEL DEPOSITION OF EXPERT WITNESS

Defendant Sw Hotel, Inc. Dba Sam Wong Hotel Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Interrogatory And Document Responses; Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff And His Attorneys (Duplicate Entry - For Entry Of Tentative Ruling Only)
Off Calendar Apr-01-2015 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Interrogatory And Document Responses; Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff And His Attorneys
Off Calendar Apr-01-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
G. Randy Kasten, Esq. of Vogl Meredith Burke LLP, Tel. 415.398.0200, for defendant and moving party;
No other appearance.

Defendant's counsel stipulated to the Judge Pro Tem.
RULING: There being no objection, the Court adopted the tentative ruling in its entirety. DEFENDANT SW HOTEL, INC. DBA SAM WONG HOTEL's Motion To Compel Further Interrogatory And Document Responses; Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff And His Attorneys is granted. The court finds that plaintiff did not make a reasonable and good faith effort to obtain the information to answer discovery. See Regency Health Services, Inc. v. Superior Court (1998) 64 CA4th1496 "A party cannot plead ignorance to information which can be obtained from sources under [her] control." That includes a party's lawyer Smith v. Sup. Ct. (1961) 189 CA 2d 6, agents or employees Gordon v. Sup. Ct. (1984) 161 CA 3d 15,167-168, family members Jones v. Superior Court (1981) 119 CA 3d 534, 552 and experts who have been retained by a party and designated as a trial witness. Sigerseth v. Superior Court (1972) 23 CA 3d 427,433. See Weil and Brown, Cal Prac. Guide: Civil Procedure Before Trial (TRG 2009) 8:1051-1060 Plaintiff to serve verified responses, including all subparts, to Form Interrogatories Form #7.1, 7.2, 7.3 and Special Interrogatories #2, 10, 10, 20, 24, 25, 26, 27, and 28 no later than May 15, 2015. Request for Production of Documents: Granted. The court finds that the document requests are tied to documents identified in the form and special interrogatories. Since plaintiff is being ordered to serve verified responses to the form and special interrogatories, then she must update her response and production to the document request. The court also find that plaintiff failed to do a diligent search and make a reasonable inquiry in responding to the document request as is required by C.C.P. 2031.220-240. Plaintiff to serve verified responses produce documents to Request for Production of Documents 1, 2, 11, 12, 17 and 18 no later than May 15, 2015. Defendant's request for sanctions is granted. Plaintiff and his attorney of record SHL Law Group is to $965.00 in sanctions to SW Hotel, Inc. DBA Sam Wong Hotel and its attorneys of record, Vogl, Meredith Burke in the amount of $965.00 no later than May 15, 2015. ORDER SIGNED. Judge Pro Tem: Katherine Gallo; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Not reported.
